steps.title: Separate the known ratio
steps.content:
Rewrite the second equation to show the ratio $\frac{j}{k}$.
$$\frac{84j}{14xk}=\frac{84}{14x}\cdot \frac{j}{k}$$

steps.title: Substitute the ratio
steps.content:
Since $\frac{j}{k}=58$, substitute it into the equation.
$$\frac{84}{14x}\cdot 58=58$$
Divide both sides by $58$.
$$\frac{84}{14x}=1$$
